prompt: Create a digital festival poster with readable on-image text. Subject: lantern procession crossing a bridge — spring festival, patterned robes, evening procession, decorative lantern glow; moving in a graceful line across the bridge over dark water; varied but rhythmically staged figures; joyful but composed. Scene: riverside bridge scene, Edo-inspired historical festival setting, Japanese urban river district, clear spring evening, blue hour into night, water planes, distant rooflines, low decorative cloud shapes, controlled lantern reflections. World logic: the title field should remain uncluttered in the upper third, lantern reflections should rhythmically echo across the water, architecture and garments should remain period-coherent. Composition: poster illustration, vertical composition with a dominant upper title field and midframe procession band, elevated side view with graceful asymmetry, 35mm lens feel, flattened decorative depth appropriate to print language, decorative procession flow from lower-left toward upper-right, negative space: quiet upper-third field for title and subhead, aspect ratio 9:16. Lighting: key light: soft lantern glow distributed across the bridge, fill: cool evening sky wash, rim: subtle warm edge on robe silhouettes nearest lantern clusters, practicals: paper lanterns, clear evening air with restrained decorative mist shapes, material response: matte paper lanterns, patterned textiles, ink-flat water surfaces, lacquer accents, warm lanterns against cool twilight field. Style stack: manga, historical, ukiyo-e, japanese-edo, poster-layout, text-in-image, woodblock-inspired line rhythm and color separation, festive grace with calm elegance, palette: indigo, vermillion, warm lantern gold, soft cream, Edo print poster language traits: flattened depth, contour rhythm, pattern discipline, asymmetric crop elegance. On-image text: headline: Lanterns of Spring; subhead: A Night Procession of Light and Pattern; body: April 18 • River Arts Hall • Kyoto District; legal: Presented by the Museum of Seasonal Arts. Text layout: headline in upper third, body copy in lower third, legal line at footer, 8% on all sides with extra breathing room at top, en. Must include: readable title hierarchy, uncluttered upper title field, bridge procession rhythm, period-coherent garments. Avoid: modern neon signage, photo-real skin rendering, busy texture behind headline, trending on artstation, 8k. Accessibility: strong headline contrast, clear separation between title and background.

thinking_config.note: Use chat/session history for multi-turn edits so thought signatures are preserved by the SDK.

metadata.provenance: Gemini-generated or Gemini-edited output; SynthID watermark expected for generated images.
notes: Poster layout with exact typography hierarchy and cultural styling benefits from the higher-fidelity model.
